narrative: Dear [Student's Name], I know this rejection letter stings—dreaming of law school and then hearing your application didn't make the cut because our model sees a 40% chance you might not pass the bar exam feels like a gut punch, especially when you've poured so much into getting here. Picture the model as an experienced advisor who's looked at thousands of stories like yours, piecing together what sets students up for bar success, and yours painted a picture of real hurdles ahead. The biggest red flag it spotted was your Year 3 decile of 1, meaning you ranked at the very bottom of your class that year, which strongly pulled the prediction toward struggle on the bar. Right behind that was your Year 1 decile of 2, showing early grades that didn't climb high enough to build momentum. Your lower-middle family income added another layer, as it often means fewer resources for the intense prep the bar demands. Being Hispanic factored in too, reflecting patterns where systemic barriers make the path tougher for students like you. Even with a solid undergraduate GPA of 3.4 and an impressive LSAT of 36, those couldn't outweigh the downward pulls from your class performance and background. Your status as a male and full-time student rounded out the influences, fitting trends that didn't tip the scales your way. It's not that any one thing doomed you—it's this combination whispering a story of challenges the model couldn't ignore. But here's the hopeful twist: this isn't destiny; it's a snapshot urging you to target those weak spots, like boosting late-year grades or seeking extra support. You've got strengths to lean on, and with focused effort, you can rewrite this story for your next shot. We're rooting for you.
